pi_items.source: Webmin official documentation, task scripts (setup_task.sh, export_result.sh), verifier.py all reference this path. Confirmed as the standard Webmin configuration file for IP access control via https://webmin.com/docs/modules/webmin-configuration/

Task description (line 5) specifies exact requirement: '127.0.0.1' (Localhost) and '10.8.0.42' (Admin VPN). Verifier.py (lines 76, 83) validates these exact IPs are in the allow= line. 127.0.0.1 is standard localhost; 10.8.0.42 is Admin VPN per task context.

Neither '0.0.0.0' nor 'all' appear in official Webmin documentation as valid values for allow= or deny= configuration lines (https://webmin.com/docs/modules/webmin-configuration/). Valid formats are: individual IP addresses, CIDR networks, hostnames, or wildcard domains. '0.0.0.0' is used as a bind address (listening on all interfaces), not in allow=/deny= lists. The metadata field is not validated or used anywhere in the task system (verifier.py, setup scripts), making these unverifiable as actual forbidden values.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses /etc/webmin/miniserv.conf for IP access control configuration. Two IP addresses must be added to the allow= line: 127.0.0.1 (localhost) and 10.8.0.42 (Admin VPN). The forbidden_ips metadata claims are unverified as '0.0.0.0' and 'all' do not appear as valid entries in Webmin's miniserv.conf documentation and are not validated by the task verifier.
